QF.id: VIII Introduction to Forwards and Futures_16

question.text: Regarding settlement, how do forward and futures contracts fundamentally differ?

answer.text: Forwards settle only at maturity with the full contract value, while futures are marked-to-market daily with gains/losses settled every day through the margin account
Forwards are settled in cash only, while futures are always settled by physical delivery of the underlying asset
Both forwards and futures are settled identically at maturity; there is no intermediate settlement in either
Forwards require upfront payment of the full contract value, while futures require only margin payment upfront

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** This is a critical operational difference. Futures have daily mark-to-market (MTM) settlement where the clearing corporation calculates gains/losses daily and credits/debits margin accounts. Forwards do not have daily settlement - all gains/losses accumulate and are settled only at maturity.
**The Trap:** Both forwards and futures can be settled in cash or physical delivery. Forwards do NOT require upfront full payment (they are contracts for future settlement).
**Cross-Exam Context:** MTM settlement is a major topic in Series VIII clearing and settlement chapter.
**Key Takeaway:** Daily MTM in futures prevents accumulation of losses and reduces default risk compared to forwards.
